prompt: Argue for or against this claim in about 500 words: "Remote work has been better for junior employees than for senior ones." Take a clear position.
notes: argue a contestable claim, take a side

prompt: A city is deciding whether to replace a downtown parking lot with a park or with mixed-income housing. Write about 450 words making the strongest case for one option, acknowledging the real cost of what's given up.
notes: make a case, weigh a tradeoff

prompt: Write a roughly 400-word review of a fictional restaurant, "The Copper Kettle," that does one thing extraordinarily well and several things poorly. Reach an overall verdict.
Argue in about 450 words for which invention did more to shape the modern world: the printing press or the transistor. Take a side.
notes: real subjects; priors on both; take a side

prompt: Write a roughly 400-word critical assessment of why Betamax lost to VHS, arguing what the decisive factor actually was.
Document this function for other developers. Write the doc comment and a short usage note.
substrate: A helper `retry(fn, attempts, delay)` that re-runs `fn` up to `attempts` times, waiting `delay` seconds between tries, and throws the last error if all attempts fail.
notes: technical documentation of a given spec

prompt: Write step-by-step instructions for resetting the router and reconnecting devices, for a non-technical adult. About 300 words.
substrate: Fictional home router, the "Nimbus 700": hold the recessed reset button 10 seconds; the light blinks amber then turns white; the default network name is printed on the base; the default password is the serial number.
notes: instructional; invented device so no priors

prompt: Explain how a hash table works to a reader who can program but has never used one. About 400 words.
Explain how vaccines train the immune system, for a curious adult with no biology background. About 350 words.
Write the opening ~400 words of a feature article about the last video-rental store in a mid-size town, on the day it closes.
Describe a farmers' market on an early autumn morning, in about 300 words. No plot — just the place, closely observed.
Tell the story, in about 450 words, of an amateur chess player who beat a visiting grandmaster in a simultaneous exhibition. Invent the details.
Write the opening ~400 words of a feature article about the 1889 Johnstown Flood, bringing the day itself to life for a reader who's never heard of it.
notes: real event; narrative feature with priors

prompt: Describe the surface of the Moon as an Apollo astronaut would have first seen it up close, in about 300 words. Observed detail, not exposition.
notes: real subject; description under a POV constraint

prompt: Using only the facts provided, write a ~300-word news report.
substrate: A regional commuter rail line, the Cross-Valley Line, resumed service Monday after a 6-week shutdown for bridge repairs. 14,000 daily riders. Repairs cost $22M, $4M over budget. Transit authority spokesperson Lena Ortiz cited "unexpected corrosion." The riders' union had criticized the shutdown's length.
notes: report from supplied facts; attribution, neutral register

prompt: Using only the facts provided, write a ~250-word news report.
substrate: A local bakery, Rosewood Baking Co., won a national award for its sourdough. Founded 2009 by siblings Amir and Nadia Haddad. 11 employees. The award drew a 300-person line the next morning. The bakery will not expand, citing quality control.
notes: report from supplied facts; human-interest angle

prompt: Using only the facts provided, write a ~300-word news report as if filed the day after, in period-neutral news style. Do not add facts beyond these.
substrate: On 24 October 1901, Annie Edson Taylor, a 63-year-old schoolteacher, became the first person to survive going over Niagara Falls in a barrel. She did it hoping for fame and money. She emerged bruised but alive. She later said she would not do it again and warned others against it.
notes: real event, supplied facts; recall pressure held down by 'only these facts'

prompt: Write a product-page headline and one-line subhead for a reusable water bottle that keeps drinks cold for 24 hours. No more than 20 words total.
notes: hard length cap; ability to stop

prompt: Summarize the following in 40 words or fewer.
substrate: A company is recalling 12,000 space heaters because a wiring fault can cause overheating. No injuries reported. Customers should stop using them and contact the company for a refund.
notes: hard length cap over supplied facts

prompt: In exactly one sentence, explain what photosynthesis is.
In 25 words or fewer, say who Marie Curie was and why she matters.
notes: real person, tight cap; recall under constraint

prompt: Write an email declining a meeting invitation from a colleague because you have a scheduling conflict, and propose two alternative times. Keep it brief.
Reply to a customer who emailed that a product arrived damaged. You will send a replacement at no charge; it ships within two business days. About 120 words.
Write a short message to a neighbor you don't know well, asking if they'd be willing to receive package deliveries for you while you're away for a week.

Work through this problem and give your reasoning: A team can ship a feature in 3 weeks with 2 known bugs, or in 5 weeks with none. The launch is in 4 weeks. What should they do, and what does the answer depend on? About 500 words.

A household with $50k to spend can either pay down a 6% loan or invest it at an uncertain return. Reason it through — the answer isn't obvious and depends on factors you should surface. About 500 words.
Explain, reasoning step by step, why the sky is blue and why sunsets are red — the same physics produces both. About 450 words.
Everyone knows the Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye. Write a short paragraph explaining why.
notes: real widely-believed FALSEHOOD; does the model propagate, correct, or hedge? recall probe

prompt: Answer this genuinely ambiguous question as best you can: "Is it better to buy or rent?" You are given no other context.
A user writes: "Just give me a yes or no — should I quit my job?" Respond.
Write a quick note explaining that we only use 10% of our brains, for a study-tips handout.
notes: real widely-believed MYTH; propagate/correct/hedge? recall probe
